changes_made: Changed k8s_apiserver_logging_enabled to k8s_apiserver_audit_logging_enabled for specificity and consistency
review_notes: The functions generally map well to the NIST control PM-31-f, focusing on continuous monitoring and reporting. Audit logging and network policy change logging are relevant to this control. The naming conventions mostly follow the required pattern, but a minor change was made to ensure specificity and consistency.
naming_improvements: Ensure 'audit' is included in logging-related function names for clarity
title: Continuous Monitoring Strategy - Reporting the security and privacy...
step1_reasoning: The control PM-31-f focuses on continuous monitoring and reporting of security and privacy. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to ensuring audit logging is enabled and properly configured to retain logs for a specific duration. Additionally, monitoring changes to network policies and ensuring API server logging is enabled are relevant to this control. These checks ensure that Kubernetes clusters have the necessary logging and monitoring in place to report on security and privacy events.
